description:
About the role
Software:
We are looking for a
Software Engineer
specializing in
automation
to design, develop, and optimize software solutions for industrial automation, robotics, and smart manufacturing systems. The ideal candidate will have expertise in
PLC programming, PC base, robotics software, and control systems
. This role involves working closely with electrical and mechanical engineers to create efficient, scalable, and reliable automation software.
Electrical:
We are seeking a skilled
Electrical Engineer
specializing in
automation
to design, develop, and maintain electrical control systems for industrial automation applications. The ideal candidate will have expertise in PLC programming, motor controls, robotics, HMI/SCADA systems, and industrial communication protocols. This role involves collaborating with cross-functional teams to optimize manufacturing processes, troubleshoot automation systems, and ensure compliance with industry standards.
What you'll be doing
Software:
Design and implement
PLC & HMI/SCADA applications
(Omron, Siemens TIA Portal, Allen-Bradley Studio 5000, etc.).
Write
Python, C++, C#, or Java
scripts for automation tasks and data processing.
Work with
ROS (Robot Operating System)
for robotic automation.
Troubleshoot and debug automation software issues.
Work with cross-functional teams (electrical, mechanical, and production engineers).
Maintain
version control
and technical documentation.
Electrical:
Design electrical control systems, schematics, and wiring diagrams for automation projects.
Work with
industrial robots (Fanuc, ABB, KUKA, etc.)
and motion control systems.
Troubleshoot and debug electrical and automation systems.
Conduct system testing, validation, and commissioning of automation equipment.
Ensure compliance with
safety standards (NFPA, NEC, IEC, UL)
.
Perform
FAT (Factory Acceptance Testing)
and
SAT (Site Acceptance Testing)
.
Collaborate with mechanical engineers, software developers, and operations teams.
Manage project timelines, budgets, and documentation.
Provide technical support for maintenance and upgrades.
What we're looking for
Software:
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Mechatronics, or a related technical field
Strong programming skills in languages such as C#, PLC
Experience with
PLC programming (Ladder Logic, Function Block, ST)
.
Excellent problem-solving and critical-thinking skills
Good communication and collaboration abilities
Passion for staying up-to-date with the latest industry trends and technologies
Electrical:
Education:
Bachelor’s degree in
Electrical Engineering, Mechatronics, or Automation Engineering
.
Knowledge of
CAD tools (AutoCAD Electrical, EPLAN, SolidWorks Electrical)
.
Familiarity with
sensors, actuators, servo systems, and pneumatics
.
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.
Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.
What we offer
At NIXMA TECHNOLOGY', we are committed to providing our employees with a rewarding and fulfilling work environment. We offer competitive salaries, comprehensive benefits, and excellent opportunities for professional development and career growth. Our employees enjoy a range of perks, including flexible working arrangements, wellness programs, and team-building activities that foster a strong sense of community.
About us
NIXMA TECHNOLOGY' is a leading provider of innovative software solutions, serving clients across a variety of industries. Our mission is to empower businesses with cutting-edge technology that drives efficiency, productivity, and growth. With a team of passionate and talented professionals, we are constantly pushing the boundaries of what's possible in the world of software engineering.
Apply now to join our dynamic team and be a part of the exciting journey at NIXMA TECHNOLOGY'.
